name: Granátula de Calatrava y Moral de Calatrava — circular desde Almagro
description: Cycle 28.1 miles through the Campo de Calatrava, exploring volcanic landscapes and historic towns like Almagro and Granátula de Calatrava.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Almagro, located in the heart of La Mancha, Spain, offers a diverse landscape for cyclists. The region is characterized by gently sloping plains, with routes that traverse both paved and unpaved surfaces. Notable natural features include unique volcanic lagoons and the nearby Tablas de Daimiel National Park, a significant wetland ecosystem. The terrain provides a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging paths, often integrating historical sites and cultural landmarks. Best touring cycling routes around Almagro The most popular touring cycling route is Presa de Jabalón — circular desde Almagro, a 30.2 miles (48.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete, featuring moderate elevation gains. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Granátula de Calatrava y Moral de Calatrava — circular desde Almagro, a moderate 28.1 miles (45.2 km) path. This route offers views of the La Mancha plains and connects historical towns. Local touring cyclists also love the Virgen de los Santos – Silo (Almagro) loop from Almagro, a 38.8 miles (62.4 km) trail leading through the wider Almagro countryside, often completed in about 5 hours 15 minutes. Touring cycling around Almagro is defined by its expansive La Mancha plains, historical towns, and proximity to unique volcanic lagoons. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy rides to more demanding routes with significant ascents. The routes in Almagro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Almagro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Almagro?
A: The Almagro region, situated in the heart of La Mancha, offers a diverse mix of terrain. You'll find routes that traverse both paved surfaces, ideal for smoother rides, and more challenging unpaved segments. The landscape is characterized by gently sloping plains, with some routes featuring significant ascents, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Almagro?
A: Yes, Almagro offers some easier routes. For instance, the region includes paths like the Almanzora Cycle Street Loop, which is an easy 17.3-mile route following river banks. While specific easy routes are fewer, the overall network provides options for less experienced cyclists, often on flatter, paved sections.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see while cycling in Almagro?
A: Many cycling routes in Almagro integrate historical sites, allowing you to explore the rich heritage of the area. The wider Campo de Calatrava region is dotted with castles, palaces, and towns of historical importance. You might encounter places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5684989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clavería Palace (Aldea del Rey)&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Doña Berenguela&lt;/a&gt;. The town of Almagro itself is a historic gem.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Almagro?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Almagro are circular, making them convenient for starting and ending in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058505797/presa-de-jabalon-circular-desde-almagro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Jabalón — circular desde Almagro&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 48.6 km route, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056883816/granatula-de-calatrava-y-moral-de-calatrava-circular-desde-almagro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granátula de Calatrava y Moral de Calatrava — circular desde Almagro&lt;/a&gt;, which is 45.2 km long.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore by bike near Almagro?
A: Almagro is close to several unique natural attractions. Just a few kilometers away is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5848229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tablas de Daimiel National Park&lt;/a&gt;, a significant wetland ecosystem that can be explored by bicycle. The municipality is also notable for its proximity to volcanic lagoons, such as Argamasilla and Inesperada, which offer unique ecological interest and scenic beauty.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Almagro?
A: The touring cycling routes in Almagro cater to a range of abilities. Out of approximately 20 routes, the majority (15) are classified as moderate, offering a good balance for most cyclists. There are also 4 difficult routes for those seeking a challenge, and at least 1 easy route for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about cycling in Almagro?
A: The touring cycling routes around Almagro are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the expansive La Mancha plains, the integration of historical towns, and the proximity to unique natural features like volcanic lagoons, making for a varied and engaging experience.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging touring cycling routes in the Almagro area?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists looking for a challenge, there are longer and more demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14657074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen de los Santos – Silo (Almagro) loop from Almagro&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 62.4 km trail that typically takes over 5 hours to complete. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14657382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Pedro (Almagro) – The House of the Virgin loop from Almagro&lt;/a&gt;, which is 40.5 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find shorter touring cycling routes for a quick ride in Almagro?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a shorter ride, the region offers options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19409263" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Membrilleja – Silo (Almagro) loop from Almagro&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate route is approximately 24.2 km long and can be completed in about 2 hours, making it suitable for a quicker excursion.

Q: Are there any specific greenways or dedicated cycle paths in the Almagro region?
A: Yes, the region features greenways that are well-suited for cycling. The Vía Verde de Almendricos is a notable example, offering a 39.4 km loop that provides a pleasant and often traffic-free cycling experience. These paths are excellent for enjoying the scenery and historical elements of the area.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on touring cycling routes around Almagro?
A: Cycling around Almagro offers expansive views of the characteristic La Mancha plains. You'll also enjoy pleasant riverside views along paths like the Almanzora Cycle Street Loop, and scenic beauty around the unique volcanic lagoons. Many routes also provide vistas of historical towns and agricultural landscapes.
tips: In the center of the old town. Rectangular, irregular floor plan, formed by two flanks with arcades of Tuscan stone columns under two running galleries, originally public in nature and open to the outside, and currently closed by a Central European style glazing that makes it unique in Spain. Located at the confluence of the main communication routes.
Museumized volcano of Campo de Calatrava. The last eruption of the volcano was 700,000 years ago, it has a cone of 300 hectares and the lava flows reached 3 kilometers.

This hermitage with a majestic façade combines the Neo-Mudejar style with the NeoclassicalE. LONELINESS.
It was ordered to be built by Juan José Moreno Palancas, illustrious son of Moral de Calatrava who was Governor of Cuba.
The works finished in 1890, but it was not opened to worship until 1994, when the Brotherhood of Soledad and Veracruz took over.

faqs.question: Where does the Granátula de Calatrava y Moral de Calatrava cycling route start?
faqs.answer: This circular route conveniently starts and ends in the historic town of Almagro. You can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of Almagro, making it easy to begin your cycling adventure.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route traverses the diverse landscapes of the Campo de Calatrava region. You can expect a mix of moorlands, undulating roads, and agricultural fields with cereals, vineyards, and olive groves. While much of the route is likely on tracks and minor roads, be prepared for some unpaved or more challenging sections, as it's a touring bicycle route.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of natural beauty and historical sites. You'll pass through towns like Granátula de Calatrava and Moral de Calatrava. Highlights include the historic Almagro Main Square, the unique volcanic landscape around Cerro Gordo Volcano, and the serene Hermitage of the Virgin of Solitude. The region also has strong connections to the Order of Calatrava and the tales of Don Quixote.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The most pleasant times to cycle this route are typically spring and autumn. During these seasons, temperatures are more moderate, making for a more comfortable experience. Summers can be very hot in this region, and winters can be chilly.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is graded as moderate, covering approximately 45 kilometers with around 300 meters of elevation gain. It requires good physical fitness and some experience with touring cycling, especially given potential unpaved sections. While not extreme, it might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through towns like Granátula de Calatrava and Moral de Calatrava, you will find opportunities for refreshments, cafes, and restaurants to refuel during your ride. Almagro, as your starting and ending point, also offers numerous options.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, this is a public cycling route, and you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to access it.

faqs.question: Is the Granátula de Calatrava y Moral de Calatrava route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, cycling routes are considered dog-friendly, especially if your dog is accustomed to cycling alongside you or can comfortably ride in a bike trailer. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved, on a leash when appropriate, and that you carry enough water for both of you.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Camino de Bocapucheros and the Camino de la Quebrada, among others, for short stretches.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a touring cycling trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and moderate difficulty, it's advisable to pack essentials such as sufficient water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit (including a spare tube), sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), and appropriate cycling attire. A fully charged phone and a map or GPS device are also recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots on the route?
faqs.answer: The entire Campo de Calatrava region offers scenic views, particularly its unique volcanic landscapes and vast agricultural fields. Keep an eye out for elevated sections that provide panoramic vistas of the rolling countryside, especially around the volcanic features like Cerro Gordo Volcano.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of the Virgin of Solitude
